title: Gravity and Grace
author: Simone Weil
description: A collection of Weil’s notebooks reflecting on attention, suffering, and spirituality—dense but illuminating philosophical fragments.

title: Man and His Symbols
author: Carl G. Jung
description: Jung’s accessible introduction to analytical psychology, symbolism, and the language of dreams, compiled with his closest collaborators.

title: The Manager's Path
author: Camille Fournier
description: A practical guide for engineers moving into management, covering feedback, coaching, team health, and the day-to-day realities of technical leadership.

title: The Staff Engineer's Path
author: Tanya Reilly
description: A guide for individual contributors navigating staff-level work, with focus on scope, influence, technical direction, and leadership without direct authority.

title: A Philosophy of Software Design
author: John Ousterhout
description: A guide to software design that focuses on reducing complexity through deeper modules, clearer interfaces, and disciplined design choices.

title: The Brothers Karamazov
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: Dostoevsky’s philosophical novel explores faith, doubt, morality, and free will through the turbulent relationships of the Karamazov family, culminating in a dramatic murder trial that questions justice and human responsibility.

title: Notes from the Underground
author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
description: Dostoevsky’s novella follows an isolated former civil servant whose bitter, self-contradictory narration cuts against social optimism and rational self-interest.

title: Moby-Dick
author: Herman Melville
description: Melville’s novel follows Ishmael and the obsessive Captain Ahab on a perilous voyage to hunt the white whale Moby Dick, exploring themes of obsession, fate, humanity, and the power of nature.
